summary: Comprehensive testing of Adjudication Gateway, Managerial Hold, and Examiner Workspace features completed. All 13 backend tests passed (100%). All frontend features tested successfully including Settings Gateway tab, ClaimDetail tier badges, Examiner Workspace, Hold modal, and Dashboard held claims banner.
passed_tests: ✅ Backend: GET /api/settings/adjudication-gateway returns default tier config {tier1_auto_pilot_limit, tier2_audit_hold_limit, enabled}
✅ Backend: PUT /api/settings/adjudication-gateway saves new tier thresholds and returns them
✅ Backend: POST /api/claims — claims under tier1 threshold get tier_level=1 (auto-pilot) with approved status
✅ Backend: POST /api/claims — claims between tier1 and tier2 get tier_level=2 with audit_flag='post_payment_audit'
✅ Backend: POST /api/claims — claims over tier2 threshold get tier_level=3 with status='pending_review' (hard hold)
✅ Backend: PUT /api/claims/{id}/hold places claim on managerial_hold with reason_code and hold_info
✅ Backend: PUT /api/claims/{id}/release-hold restores previous status (admin only)
✅ Backend: POST /api/claims/{id}/force-preventive overrides claim to preventive $0 member cost
✅ Backend: POST /api/claims/{id}/adjust-deductible changes member_responsibility
✅ Backend: POST /api/claims/{id}/carrier-notification flags carrier_notification=true
✅ Backend: GET /api/reports/fixed-cost-vs-claims excludes claims with managerial_hold status (Bordereaux exclusion)
✅ Backend: GET /api/users returns list of users (admin only)
✅ Backend: Dashboard metrics include held_claims count and pending_review in pending_claims
✅ Frontend: Settings page shows Adjudication Gateway tab with 3 tier cards and save button
✅ Frontend: Settings page Tier 1/2/3 cards display correct colors (green/amber/red) and editable thresholds
✅ Frontend: ClaimDetail shows tier badge (Tier 1/2/3) with correct colors
✅ Frontend: ClaimDetail shows audit flag badge ('Post-Payment Audit') when applicable
✅ Frontend: ClaimDetail shows Examiner Workspace with plan-type-specific actions (MEC: ACA Preventive Validator)
✅ Frontend: ClaimDetail shows Hold button and Hold modal with reason code dropdown (5 options)
✅ Frontend: ClaimDetail shows Hold banner when claim is on managerial_hold with release button
✅ Frontend: Claims list shows managerial_hold ('On Hold') and pending_review ('Pending Review') statuses in filter dropdown
✅ Frontend: Dashboard shows held claims banner when held_claims > 0 with 'Review' button
✅ Frontend: Dashboard Claims by Status chart includes 'Managerial Hold' and 'Pending Review' categories
seed_data_creation: Test members created during testing (TEST_GATEWAY_*) linked to MEC 1 plan for tier logic and examiner action tests
context_for_next_testing_agent: Adjudication Gateway, Managerial Hold, and Examiner Workspace features are fully functional. Key behaviors: (1) Gateway tier logic: Tier 1 (≤$500) auto-paid, Tier 2 ($500-$2500) paid + post-payment audit flag, Tier 3 (>$2500) pending_review status. (2) Hold feature: PUT /claims/{id}/hold with reason_code places claim on managerial_hold, stores hold_info with previous_status, placed_by, placed_at. Release requires admin role. (3) Examiner Workspace: MEC plans show ACA Preventive Validator with Force Preventive button, Standard plans show Deductible/OOP Tracker and Stop-Loss carrier notification. (4) Bordereaux exclusion: Claims on managerial_hold are excluded from /reports/fixed-cost-vs-claims aggregation. (5) Dashboard: Shows held_claims banner and pending_review count in metrics. (6) Settings: Gateway tab with 3 tier cards (green/amber/red), editable thresholds, enabled toggle.
